manjang.name: Manjanggul Lava Tube (UNESCO World Natural Heritage · Lava Tube)
manjang.category: UNESCO World Natural Heritage — Geomunoreum Lava-Tube System (inscribed 2007)
manjang.description:
**Manjanggul Lava Tube (Manjanggul Lava Tube)** is a lava tube formed by **lava that erupted roughly 8,000 years ago from Geomunoreum, a parasitic volcano on the flank of Hallasan**, and in **2007 it was inscribed on the UNESCO World Natural Heritage list together with Seongsan Ilchulbong and Hallasan under the name 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes'**. Because it belongs to the **same single inscribed property** as Seongsan Ilchulbong, this tour packs **two World Natural Heritage sites** into a single day. 

With a total length of about **7.4 km** (13–15 km including connected sections), it is one of the largest lava tubes in the world, and its main passage reaches **18 m wide and 23–25 m high**. Of the three entrances created by ceiling collapse, **only about a 1 km stretch from the second entrance is open to the public**, making it an **out-and-back course of about 30–45 min** in which you return the way you came. At the end of the open section stands the **world's tallest lava column at 7.6 m high**, Manjanggul's iconic photo spot. 

The inside of the cave stays at **11–13°C year-round**, cool in summer and mild in winter, but the floor is dark and somewhat damp, so we recommend **a jacket and non-slip shoes**. It is also designated a **National Natural Monument**. Admission is **₩4,000 for adults (₩3,000 for groups) / ₩2,000 for teens and children**, hours are **09:00–18:00 (last entry 17:10)**, closed the first Wednesday of every month.
highlights: **UNESCO World Natural Heritage 2007** — inscribed under 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' alongside Seongsan Ilchulbong
**Two World Natural Heritage sites in one day** — Seongsan Ilchulbong + Manjanggul
Total length about **7.4 km**, open section about **1 km** (about 30–45 min round trip)
**World's largest lava column, 7.6 m high**, at the end of the open section
Main passage **18 m wide · 23–25 m high** — one of the world's largest lava tubes
**Geomunoreum Lava-Tube System** — formed about 8,000 years ago, a National Natural Monument
Interior **11–13°C year-round** — a summer cool-down, jacket recommended
Admission **₩4,000 adult / ₩2,000 teens and children**, hours **09:00–18:00 (last entry 17:10)**
timeUsed: Descent into the second entrance + entering the cave — about 5 min
1 km open-section round trip (to the lava column) — about 30 min
Lava-tube formation talk at the information center — about 10 min
manjang.whyOnRoute: **The day's second UNESCO World Natural Heritage site and its natural finale.** After Seongsan Ilchulbong (a triple-UNESCO tuff cone) and the haenyeo diving performance (intangible heritage), Manjanggul in Gimnyeong sits naturally on the route back from Seongsan to Jeju City. Because it is part of the **same single 2007 inscription** as Seongsan Ilchulbong, pairing the two in one day completes the UNESCO 'Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' narrative. Placing it in the late afternoon **avoids the midday heat and group crowds**, and ends the day on a pleasant note in a **cool 11–13°C cave** after the outdoor itinerary. Should Manjanggul be closed for maintenance or weather, we substitute **Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land**, part of the same lava-tube system, to preserve the cave experience.

smartNotes.photo: The **world's largest lava column at 7.6 m high** at the end of the open section is the signature shot, and a perspective image down the long, 18 m wide, 23 m high passage is overwhelming in scale.
smartNotes.facilities: The information center beside the entrance parking lot explains how the lava tube formed — we recommend a 10-minute look before entering. There is no shop inside the cave.
smartNotes.tip: Bring a jacket even in summer (interior 11–13°C). The floor is damp and dark, so non-slip shoes are best. The cave can close for maintenance, so check whether it is operating on your visit date in advance.

visitBasics.closed: First Wednesday of every month / temporary closure during heavy rain
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — flat 1 km round trip, stair descent at the cave entrance, somewhat uneven and damp floor

convenience.restroom: Restrooms at the entrance information center
convenience.parking: Free parking lot at the entrance

haenyeo.name: Haenyeo Diving Performance (Haenyeo Museum if the performance is canceled)
haenyeo.category: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity — Culture of Jeju Haenyeo (2016), live
haenyeo.description:
**Jeju Haenyeo (women divers) culture** is a living heritage inscribed on the **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity list in 2016**. On this tour you don't watch a museum display but **a real haenyeo diving performance staged in the sea below Seongsan Ilchulbong**. **Elderly, still-active haenyeo** who have made the sea their workplace their whole lives free-dive without oxygen gear, relying on a *tewak* (float), and you can hear for yourself the distinctive whistling sumbisori breath they release when they finish a dive. 

The performance usually runs **twice a day (around 13:30 and 15:00)**, depending on sea and weather conditions, and **viewing is free**. Haenyeo are divided into **hagun, junggun, and sanggun** by depth and diving ability, with the sanggun descending **more than 10 m on a single breath**. Fewer than **4,000 haenyeo are active today, most of them over 70**, so every scene of diving you witness in person is a record of a vanishing heritage. 

**On days when the performance is canceled due to weather or waves**, we substitute the **Jeju Haenyeo Museum** in Hado-ri, Gujwa-eup (₩1,100 for adults, about 80% indoors, three exhibition halls), continuing the same UNESCO intangible heritage in depth, indoors.
highlights: **UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity (2016)** as a **live diving performance**, not a museum
Watch active haenyeo actually free-dive **in the sea below Seongsan Ilchulbong**
No oxygen tank, just a single *tewak* — the sanggun dive **more than 10 m on one breath**
Hear the distinctive **sumbisori** breath at the end of each dive in person
Performance usually **twice a day (around 13:30 and 15:00)**, **free viewing**, subject to weather and sea conditions
Fewer than **4,000 active haenyeo, most over 70** — a vanishing heritage
**Haenyeo Museum substitute if the performance is canceled** (₩1,100 adult, 80% indoors, three exhibition halls)
**5 min by foot or vehicle** from Seongsan Ilchulbong — a natural link with no loss of route time
timeUsed: Transfer from Seongsan Ilchulbong to the performance site — about 5 min
Performance briefing + taking a spot — about 5 min
Watching the haenyeo diving performance — about 30 min
haenyeo.whyOnRoute: **The climax where you meet UNESCO intangible heritage as a 'live event' rather than an 'exhibit'.** Right after climbing and viewing Seongsan Ilchulbong, the route flows naturally into the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea of the same area (5-min transfer). Seeing the tuff cone's volcanic geology (World Natural Heritage), then immediately seeing the intangible heritage (2016) of the haenyeo who have lived from that sea, completes the eastern route's distinctive 'volcano + people' narrative. The performance is free and usually held in the afternoon, fitting the route well. **However, it can be canceled depending on the sea and weather**, so on such days we substitute the indoor Haenyeo Museum in Hado-ri to **guarantee the haenyeo heritage experience in any weather**.

smartNotes.photo: The silhouette of a haenyeo surfacing while clinging to her tewak, and a sea shot with Seongsan Ilchulbong in the background, are iconic.
smartNotes.facilities: Restrooms and parking near the performance site / when canceled, use the Haenyeo Museum's café, gift shop, and English-language information.
smartNotes.tip: Performance times vary with the day's sea conditions, so the guide will advise on site. A hat and sunglasses for sun protection are recommended.

visitBasics.hours: Performance usually 13:30 and 15:00 (varies with the day's sea and weather) / Haenyeo Museum 09:00–18:00 (last entry 17:00)
visitBasics.closed: Performance: canceled in bad weather or waves / Museum: closed Mondays, January 1, Lunar New Year, Chuseok
visitBasics.admission: Diving performance viewing free / if substituted, Haenyeo Museum adult ₩1,100 · teens ₩800 · children ₩500
visitBasics.walking: Easy — viewed from the shore; if substituted with the museum, fully indoor with elevators

convenience.restroom: Near the performance site / many inside the museum
convenience.parking: Free parking at both the performance site and the museum

lunchDescription: About a 60-minute lunch break between Seongeup Folk Village and Seongsan Ilchulbong. At restaurants around Seongsan-eup harbor you can explore eastern Jeju local fare such as black pig, abalone hot pot, and cutlassfish. Lunch is not included (about ₩15,000).

whyOnRoute.hamdeok: **The morning's first stop, opening with the sea.** Right after pickup at four points across Jeju City, the day begins at Hamdeok Seoubong Beach, about a 40-minute drive away. After lightly enjoying the emerald water, white sand, and the walking heritage of Olle Trail Course 19, the route continues inland to Seongeup and on to the eastern tip at Seongsan and Manjanggul. Because it is early in the morning, it is relatively quiet even in the summer peak season.
whyOnRoute.seongeup: **The day's only inland Joseon-era heritage site and its geographic pivot point.** Located on the way from the Hamdeok coast toward Seongsan (inland Pyoseon), it turns plain driving time into a meaningful stop. The stone walls and thatched roofs of this National Folklore Cultural Heritage village and its 600-year-old old trees add a 'leisurely cultural walk' that contrasts with the afternoon's volcanic and haenyeo heritage, balancing the day so it is not just a pilgrimage of photo spots.
whyOnRoute.lunch: **A recharge right before the core UNESCO itinerary.** At a local restaurant on the road from Seongeup to Seongsan, you have lunch of eastern Jeju local fare such as black pig, abalone hot pot, and seasonal seafood (meal not included). It is the spot to fill up before the most active afternoon — climbing Seongsan, the haenyeo diving performance, and Manjanggul.
whyOnRoute.seongsan: **The first of the day's two World Natural Heritage sites.** After lunch you climb Seongsan Ilchulbong (optional climb, about 30 min round trip) to see the tuff cone that rose from an underwater eruption about 5,000 years ago. The climb leads straight into the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea of the same area, then wraps up at the Manjanggul lava tube — part of the same 2007 inscription — completing the 'Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' narrative in a single day.

routePhases.description: Pickup at four points across Jeju City (08:30–09:05), then transfer to Hamdeok Seoubong Beach.

routePhases.phase: From the coast to inland cultural heritage
routePhases.description: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (60 min) → Seongeup Folk Village (Joseon-era National Folklore Cultural Heritage, 60 min).
Local restaurant near Seongsan — black pig, abalone hot pot, seasonal seafood. Lunch not included.
Seongsan Ilchulbong (optional climb) → haenyeo diving performance (Haenyeo Museum if canceled) → Manjanggul lava tube (UNESCO World Natural Heritage).
Gimnyeong → transfer to Jeju City + drop-off at two points and Dongmun Market.

routeShapeSubtitle: An East Jeju loop that connects the island's two UNESCO World Natural Heritage sites in a single day from the eastern tip — Seongsan Ilchulbong (a tuff cone) and Manjanggul (a lava tube), both inscribed in 2007 as the same property. To these it adds the haenyeo diving performance, a UNESCO intangible heritage. An efficient route that avoids the crowds at the major sights.

seo.metaDescription: 9-hour East Jeju small-group loop: Hamdeok Beach, Seongeup Folk Village (UNESCO folk heritage), two World Natural Heritage sites in Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO) and Manjanggul (UNESCO), plus a haenyeo diving performance. All admissions included.
seo.description: 9-hour East Jeju small-group day tour: Hamdeok Beach → Seongeup Folk Village → lunch → Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO) → haenyeo diving performance → Manjanggul (UNESCO). All admissions included, certified guide.

catalog.shortCardDescription: Loop eastern Jeju's UNESCO sights in a 9-hour small group. Includes Hamdeok Seoubong Beach, Seongeup Folk Village (UNESCO folk heritage), Seongsan Ilchulbong and Manjanggul — two UNESCO World Natural Heritage sites inscribed together in 2007 — plus a haenyeo diving performance (Haenyeo Museum if canceled). All admissions included, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide.
catalog.subtitle: Small-group minibus with English/Chinese guide — loop Hamdeok Beach, Seongeup Folk Village, Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO), the haenyeo diving performance, and Manjanggul (UNESCO) in a 9-hour eastern route.

items.label: Two UNESCO World Natural Heritage sites + intangible heritage in one day
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong and Manjanggul are World Natural Heritage sites inscribed together in 2007 as 'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes'. Add the haenyeo diving performance, a 2016 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity, and you see three layers of UNESCO heritage in a single day.

items.label: The real Manjanggul — the original UNESCO lava tube, no longer a substitute
items.detail: In the past we substituted Micheon Cave during closures, but now you explore Manjanggul itself — one of the world's largest lava tubes, home to the world's largest lava column at 7.6 m high.

items.label: All admissions included — the value at this price
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000 + Manjanggul ₩4,000 (Seongeup free, haenyeo diving performance free) — two World Natural Heritage sites are covered by just ₩9,000 in admissions.

items.label: Seongsan Ilchulbong is an optional climb
items.detail: The roughly 30-minute round-trip climb to the crater rim is steep and not right for everyone. There is also a coastal loop path with a similar view and no stairs, so you can choose according to your fitness.

bookingTrust.routeBody: All admissions included in the tour fee: Seongsan Ilchulbong (₩5,000), Manjanggul (₩4,000), Seongeup Folk Village (free), haenyeo diving performance (free) — nothing extra to pay on site.
bookingTrust.groupBody: A small-group minibus (max 14) lets you explore Seongeup's alleys, the Manjanggul lava tube, and the haenyeo diving performance at a relaxed pace.

staticQuestions.question: Can I actually watch a haenyeo diving performance?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. Watching the haenyeo diving performance held in the sea near Seongsan Ilchulbong is part of the route (usually in the afternoon, free). It can be canceled depending on sea and weather conditions, and if canceled we substitute the indoor Haenyeo Museum in Hado-ri to guarantee the haenyeo heritage experience.

staticQuestions.question: Can I skip the Seongsan Ilchulbong crater climb?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. There is a coastal loop path that offers a similar photo angle without stairs. The crater climb (about 0.8 km round trip, about 30 min) is optional, so you can decide according to your fitness.

staticQuestions.question: What is the difference from the southern Jeju UNESCO tour?
staticQuestions.answer: It is a tour by the same operator. The southern tour includes a Hallasan oreum, Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon Falls, the columnar joints, and the Osulloc tea fields. The eastern tour focuses on Seongsan Ilchulbong, Seongeup Folk Village, Manjanggul, and the haenyeo diving performance.

staticQuestions.question: What happens on days when Manjanggul is closed?
staticQuestions.answer: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube included in the route. However, on days it is closed for maintenance or weather (heavy rain, etc.), we substitute a nearby cave such as Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land, part of the same lava-tube system, to keep the cave experience.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: It is not included. Lunch is separate (about ₩15,000), and the guide will recommend a good local spot.

walkingContent: Seongeup Folk Village has dirt paths and stone walls.
The Seongsan Ilchulbong crater-rim climb is about 30 min of steep stone steps (can be skipped).
The haenyeo diving performance is viewed from the flat shore.
Manjanggul is about a 1 km flat round trip, with stairs at the cave entrance and a somewhat uneven, damp floor inside (11–13°C year-round, jacket recommended).
practical.cavePreview: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube included in the route.
caveContent: Manjanggul is a UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube inscribed in 2007 and is included in this route.
The open section is about a 1 km round trip (about 30–45 min), with the world's largest lava column at 7.6 m high at the end.
The interior is a cool 11–13°C year-round, so a jacket is recommended.
If closed for maintenance or weather, it may be substituted with a nearby lava tube (Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land) — confirm when booking.
